worth_it_explanation: Budget and pacing problems in Yahoo DSP can exhaust a flight before it ends or leave significant budget unspent, neither of which triggers an obvious alert at the line level. Yahoo DSP structures spend through a Campaign / Package / Line hierarchy where campaign-level budget caps constrain every line beneath them. A line may still show budget remaining while the parent campaign cap has already stopped delivery. Catching a misaligned pacing mode, a campaign cap that is too low relative to the lines under it, or a line falling behind its even-pace target early in the flight are each a settings review, not a rebuild.
intro: Yahoo DSP organizes spend through an Advertiser / Campaign / Package / Line hierarchy. Budget and pacing settings live at the campaign level and flow down to lines; campaign-level budgets override or cap line-level delivery. Pacing controls how spend distributes across a flight: 'Even' spreads the budget in roughly equal increments day by day, while 'Accelerated' front-loads spend as quickly as available inventory allows. When the pacing mode does not match the flight goal, when a campaign budget cap is set too low relative to the lines beneath it, or when a line falls behind its even-pace reference early in a flight, the result is either a burst of delivery followed by silence, or underspend that cannot be recovered in the final days. These checks confirm that pacing and budget settings are deliberate and coherent across the campaign hierarchy.

audit_checks.title: Pacing mode does not match the campaign goal or flight length
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A campaign using Accelerated pacing on a multi-week brand-awareness or reach flight spends the budget in the first days and then goes dark for the remainder of the flight. The impression count is delivered, but it is concentrated in a short window rather than distributed across the period where the advertiser actually wanted presence. The result is a flight that appears to complete successfully but delivers far less sustained reach than the budget should have supported.

educate.base:
Yahoo DSP (demand-side platform) controls how spend distributes across a flight through the pacing mode setting, configured at the campaign level. The two primary modes are 'Even' and 'Accelerated'.

'Even' spreads the budget in roughly equal increments across each day of the flight, targeting a consistent daily delivery rate from the first day to the last. 'Accelerated' front-loads spend, bidding as aggressively as available inventory allows early in the flight and reducing pace only as the budget approaches exhaustion. A daily budget setting adds a separate ceiling that applies regardless of the pacing mode.

The choice between the two modes depends on the campaign goal and the flight length. 'Accelerated' is suited to short, time-critical campaigns where the full budget must go out quickly, such as a one-day sale event or a breaking-news sponsorship. 'Even' is suited to a sustained awareness or reach campaign where consistent daily presence across a multi-week or multi-month flight matters more than speed of delivery.

A few signs suggest the pacing mode may not match the goal:
  • A brand-awareness or reach campaign set to Accelerated, which concentrates impressions in the first days and leaves most of the flight without delivery
  • A time-sensitive promotion set to Even where the daily pace target falls short of the volume the campaign needs to deliver before the offer expires
  • A campaign showing a steep delivery curve early in the flight followed by a flat line, with budget exhausted well before the flight end date
You may want to review the pacing mode against the flight length and the campaign goal before the flight begins rather than after most of the budget has already spent.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a store running a promotional flight tied to a sale window, Accelerated pacing can align well with peak purchase intent if the flight is short and the full budget is intended to spend before the sale ends. You may want to confirm the flight end date matches the promotion end date so spend does not continue after the sale has closed.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser running a sustained flight without a hard event deadline, Even pacing typically produces more consistent lead volume across the flight period. You may want to confirm that the daily delivery target under Even pacing is achievable given the targeting, bid, and available inventory on the lines.

educate.learn_more_note: Authoritative documentation on Yahoo DSP pacing mode options and their delivery behaviors is available in the Yahoo DSP Help Center, which requires a Yahoo DSP account login to access (help.yahooinc.com/dsp). Contact your Yahoo DSP account team for guidance on pacing mode selection if you do not have Help Center access.

investigate.intro: Confirm the pacing mode set on each active campaign, then assess whether it matches the campaign goal and the remaining flight duration.

yahoo_dsp_ui.label: Yahoo DSP UI
steps: Select 'Advertisers' in the left navigation pane and choose the advertiser you are auditing.
On the Campaigns page, open each active campaign. Navigate to the campaign's budget and pacing settings. Note the pacing mode ('Even' or 'Accelerated') and the daily budget setting if one is applied.
Review the delivery chart for the campaign. Note whether spend front-loaded heavily in the first days of the flight or tracks closer to a consistent daily rate.

Compare the pacing mode to the campaign goal and the flight length. A multi-week reach or awareness campaign using Accelerated pacing is a candidate for review. A short promotional campaign using Even pacing where the daily delivery target may not be achievable before the offer ends is also a candidate.
Note the flight start and end dates, the total campaign budget, the pacing mode, and the observed delivery pattern for each campaign you flag.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per active campaign, the pacing mode, the flight length, and the campaign goal. A campaign whose pacing mode aligns with the goal and flight length passes. A campaign using Accelerated pacing on a flight longer than a few days where sustained daily presence is the goal, or using Even pacing on a short time-critical flight where the daily pace target is not achievable, is a fail recorded with the campaign name, pacing mode, flight length, and the misalignment identified.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the pacing mode, flight length, campaign goal, and delivery pattern for each active campaign above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more campaigns in {{site_name}}'s Yahoo DSP (demand-side platform) account use a pacing mode that does not match the campaign goal or flight structure. A campaign using Accelerated pacing on a multi-week flight concentrates impressions in the opening days and then goes dark, delivering the budget without the sustained daily presence the flight was designed to provide. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the pacing mode on each active campaign against the campaign goal and flight length, and switching to Even pacing for sustained awareness or reach flights where consistent daily delivery across the full period is the intent.

audit_checks.title: Campaign budget cap is set too low relative to line budgets, cutting delivery without a clear alert
audit_checks.impact_rationale: When a campaign budget cap is lower than the sum of its lines' intended spend, Yahoo DSP stops delivering all lines once the campaign cap is reached, even when individual lines still have budget remaining. The constraint is not surfaced as a line-level error, so the account appears healthy at the line level while campaign-level delivery has already stopped. The result is a flight that under-delivers against its plan without a visible warning on the lines themselves.

educate.base:
Yahoo DSP (demand-side platform) organizes spend in a Campaign / Package / Line hierarchy. Campaign-level budgets act as a ceiling for all spend within the campaign: once the campaign budget is exhausted, every line beneath it stops delivering, regardless of how much budget each individual line has remaining.

A configuration problem arises when the campaign budget is set too low relative to the total spend the lines beneath it are expected to generate. This can happen when a campaign manager adds new lines or increases line budgets without adjusting the parent campaign budget, or when the campaign budget is reduced mid-flight without also reducing the line budgets beneath it. In either case, the campaign cap becomes the binding constraint that stops delivery before the lines reach their own budgets.

The situation is easy to miss because the line-level view shows remaining line budget and does not immediately surface how close the parent campaign is to its cap. The signal that the campaign cap was the constraint appears in the campaign-level delivery chart: spend flattens or stops while lines beneath still show budget remaining and no line-level error is raised.

A few signs point to this condition:
  • All lines within a campaign stop delivering at approximately the same time, with no individual line reporting budget exhaustion
  • The campaign delivery chart shows spend flattening at a point below the campaign's flight end date while lines still show budget remaining
  • A line was added or its budget was increased without a corresponding increase to the campaign budget
You may want to review the campaign budget alongside the sum of its active line budgets at the start of each flight and after any line budget change.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a store running multiple lines targeting different products or audience segments under one campaign, a campaign cap reached early routes no further spend to any line, including lines for high-priority products that still have budget available. You may want to confirm the campaign budget reflects the total intended spend across all product-targeting lines.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser with several audience-targeted lines under one campaign, a campaign budget cap that cuts across lines stops all audience targeting simultaneously. You may want to confirm the campaign budget is set to accommodate the full planned spend across every active line before the flight begins.

educate.learn_more_note: Documentation on Yahoo DSP campaign budget hierarchy and how campaign caps constrain line delivery is available in the Yahoo DSP Help Center, which requires a Yahoo DSP account login to access (help.yahooinc.com/dsp). The Microsoft Learn guide on targeting Yahoo DSP inventory also confirms that every line inherits default budget settings from its parent campaign.

investigate.intro: Confirm that the campaign budget is set at or above the sum of its active line budgets for the same flight period, and check whether the campaign delivery history shows a premature cap.

yahoo_dsp_ui.label: Yahoo DSP UI
steps: Select 'Advertisers' in the left navigation pane and choose the advertiser you are auditing.
On the Campaigns page, open each active campaign. Note the campaign budget amount and the campaign flight dates.
Navigate to the Lines page for the campaign. For each active line, note its budget amount and confirm the line flight dates fall within the campaign flight dates.
Add the line budget amounts together and compare the sum to the campaign budget. Note whether the sum of line budgets exceeds the campaign cap.

Open the campaign-level delivery chart. If the delivery line flattens or drops to zero before the flight end date while individual lines still show budget remaining, the campaign cap was the binding constraint during that period. Record the campaign name, the campaign budget, and the sum of line budgets for any campaign where the cap appears to have cut delivery early.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per campaign, the campaign budget and the sum of its active line budgets for the same flight period. A campaign whose budget equals or exceeds the sum of its active line budgets passes. A campaign whose line budgets sum to more than the campaign cap, or whose delivery chart shows spend stopping before the flight end while lines still carry budget, is a fail recorded with the campaign name, the campaign budget, and the line budget total.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the campaign budget and the sum of active line budgets for each campaign above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more campaigns in {{site_name}}'s Yahoo DSP (demand-side platform) account have a campaign budget cap set below the sum of their active line budgets, which causes Yahoo DSP to stop all line delivery once the campaign cap is reached. Individual lines show budget remaining, but the parent campaign cap is the binding constraint that halts delivery. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the campaign budget for each affected campaign against the total of its active line budgets, and adjusting the campaign cap to accommodate the intended full-flight spend across all lines, or reducing line budgets to fit within the existing campaign cap if the total spend needs to stay fixed.

audit_checks.title: Active lines are underpacing and at risk of leaving budget unspent by the flight end date
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A line running behind its even-pace reference for most of a flight leaves budget that cannot easily be absorbed in the final days. Attempting to spend a large portion of remaining budget in a compressed window requires higher bids to win enough impressions quickly, which raises the effective cost per impression above what the flight originally planned for. The result is a combination of underspend and higher-than-expected costs on whatever portion does deliver.

educate.base:
A Yahoo DSP (demand-side platform) line underpaces when its actual spend runs behind the expected daily rate for the flight period. Yahoo DSP's even-pace calculation divides the remaining budget by the remaining days in the flight; when actual spend consistently falls short of that daily target, the line is underpacing.

Several conditions produce underpacing. Bids set too low to win enough auctions at the current targeting, targeting parameters too narrow to find sufficient inventory across Yahoo's supply, a daily budget cap on the campaign set below the amount needed to hit the even-pace target, or creative that is not eligible in the ad formats the line targets are each conditions that can cause spend to fall behind the pace reference. Yahoo DSP does not automatically raise bids or widen targeting to compensate, so underpacing that begins early in a flight will continue unless the underlying constraint is addressed.

Yahoo Blueprint Performance, Yahoo DSP's AI bidding algorithm, improves bid precision and pacing feedback loops, but it operates within the bid and targeting constraints the campaign manager sets. If the bid is below the clearing price for the targeted inventory, Blueprint Performance cannot correct the underpacing on its own.

A few signs point to a line at risk of underspending:
  • The line delivery chart shows spend running below the even-pace reference with more than a third of the flight remaining
  • The campaign has a daily budget cap that, when divided by the number of active lines, is below the amount each line needs to hit its own daily pace target
  • The line's bid is near the floor price for the targeted inventory type, leaving little room to win auctions against other buyers
You may want to review delivery pacing on active lines at least once per week and more frequently in the final third of a flight, when underpacing is hardest to recover from without raising costs.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a store with a promotional flight, underpacing in the first half of the flight means the promotion reaches fewer potential buyers than planned during the period of highest purchase intent. You may want to identify whether the underpacing traces to a bid constraint, a daily cap on the parent campaign, or a targeting constraint before adjusting. Widening targeting on a promotional flight can reduce relevance and lower conversion rates.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser with a fixed flight budget, underpacing early means fewer leads than the budget should support. You may want to confirm whether the underpacing traces to a bid that is not competitive for the target audience's inventory. Raising the bid is often a faster path to recovery than widening the audience, particularly if the audience size is already large.

educate.learn_more_note: Documentation on Yahoo DSP line-level pacing indicators, delivery reports, and troubleshooting tools for underpacing lines is available in the Yahoo DSP Help Center, which requires a Yahoo DSP account login to access (help.yahooinc.com/dsp). Yahoo DSP also provides an AI-driven troubleshooting agent that proactively identifies underpacing lines and diagnoses root causes across targeting, supply, and settings.

impactful_updates.update_title: Yahoo DSP Agentic AI: always-on underpacing troubleshooting
impactful_updates.update_description: Yahoo DSP introduced an always-on Troubleshooting Agent as part of its agentic AI capabilities. When a line underpaces, the agent diagnoses root causes across targeting, supply, and settings, then recommends, or executes with human approval, corrective actions. This reduces the window between when underpacing begins and when a practitioner identifies and addresses the cause.

investigate.intro: Confirm whether active lines are on pace to spend their budgets by the flight end date, and identify any that show meaningful underpacing relative to the expected daily rate.

yahoo_dsp_ui.label: Yahoo DSP UI
steps: Select 'Advertisers' in the left navigation pane and choose the advertiser you are auditing.

On the Campaigns page, open each active campaign and navigate to the Lines page. Review the delivery status for each active line. Lines showing spend significantly below the expected daily rate are candidates for review.
Open each underpacing line and review its delivery chart. Note the percentage of the flight budget spent, the percentage of the flight elapsed, and the days remaining in the flight.

Check the campaign-level daily budget setting. If the daily cap divided by the number of active lines is below the amount each line needs to hit its own even-pace target, the campaign daily cap may be the constraint rather than the line bid or targeting.

Review the line bid setting. Note whether the current bid is at or near the minimum allowed for the targeted inventory type and ad format. A bid near the floor is a signal that the line may not be winning enough auctions to pace.
Note the gap between actual spend and the even-pace reference, and estimate how much budget per day would need to deliver in the remaining flight to reach full spend by the end date.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per active line, the percentage of budget spent versus the percentage of flight elapsed, the daily delivery rate, and the identified cause of underpacing where applicable. A line spending within a reasonable margin of the expected daily rate passes. A line where spend is more than 15 percentage points behind the expected pace with less than half the flight remaining is a fail, recorded with the line name, the budget percentage spent, the flight percentage elapsed, and the likely cause identified during investigation.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the budget percentage spent, the flight percentage elapsed, the daily delivery rate, and the identified cause for each active line above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more active lines in {{site_name}}'s Yahoo DSP (demand-side platform) account are underpacing and at risk of leaving budget unspent by the flight end date. When a line runs behind its expected daily rate for most of a flight, the remaining budget cannot be absorbed in the final days without raising bids above what the original flight economics assumed. {{voice_recommend}} identifying the cause of underpacing on each affected line, whether a bid too low to win sufficient inventory, a campaign daily cap constraining all lines beneath it, or targeting too narrow to find enough impressions, and addressing the constraint while enough flight remains for delivery to recover at a reasonable cost.
